alternative_parents.description: Inorganic compounds of sulfur with a less electronegative element.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic sulfides

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic salt compounds containing a sodium atom either in its ionic form or bonded to another atom (metal or halogen).
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic sodium salts

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic salts containing a silver ion or a bonded silver atom.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic silver salts

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic chemical compounds with one or more oxygen atoms combined with another element.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic oxides

class.description: Inorganic oxoanionic compounds in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a transition metal.
class.name: Transition metal oxoanionic compounds

description: This compound belongs to the class of inorganic compounds known as transition metal thiosulfates. These are inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is thiosulfate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a transition metal.

direct_parent.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is thiosulfate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a transition metal.
direct_parent.name: Transition metal thiosulfates

kingdom.description: Compounds that do not contain a C atom. Exceptions are made for isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon trioxide, carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Inorganic compounds

subclass.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is thiosulfate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a transition metal.
subclass.name: Transition metal thiosulfates

superclass.description: Compounds that contains both metal and non metal atoms.
superclass.name: Mixed metal/non-metal compounds
